package.doc:

class.name: DistributedSchedulingAllocateRequest

method.name: getAllocateRequest
method.doc: Get the underlying AllocateRequest object. @return Allocate request

method.name: setAllocateRequest
method.doc: Set the underlying AllocateRequest object. @param allocateRequest Allocate request

method.name: getAllocatedContainers
setAllocatedContainers

class.doc: Object used by the Application Master when distributed scheduling is enabled, in order to forward the {@link AllocateRequest} for GUARANTEED containers to the Resource Manager, and to notify the Resource Manager about the allocation of OPPORTUNISTIC containers through the Distributed Scheduler.

class.name: DistributedSchedulingAllocateResponse
class.doc: This is the response of the Resource Manager to the {@link DistributedSchedulingAllocateRequest}, when distributed scheduling is enabled. It includes the {@link AllocateResponse} for the GUARANTEED containers allocated by the Resource Manager. Moreover, it includes a list with the nodes that can be used by the Distributed Scheduler when allocating containers.

class.name: LogAggregationReport

method.name: getApplicationId
method.doc: Get the ApplicationId of the application. @return ApplicationId of the application

method.name: getLogAggregationStatus
method.doc: Get the LogAggregationStatus. @return LogAggregationStatus

method.name: getDiagnosticMessage
method.doc: Get the diagnositic information of this log aggregation @return diagnositic information of this log aggregation

class.doc:
{@code LogAggregationReport} is a report for log aggregation status in one NodeManager of an application.

It includes details such as:

  • {@link ApplicationId} of the application.
  • {@link LogAggregationStatus}
  • Diagnostic information

class.name: RegisterDistributedSchedulingAMResponse
class.doc: This is the response to registering an Application Master when distributed scheduling is enabled. Apart from the {@link RegisterApplicationMasterResponse}, it includes various parameters to be used during distributed scheduling, such as the min and max resources that can be requested by containers.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.api.records

class.name: NodeAction
class.doc: The NodeManager is instructed to perform the given action.

class.name: NodeHealthStatus

method.name: getIsNodeHealthy
method.doc: Is the node healthy? @return true if the node is healthy, else false

method.name: getHealthReport
method.doc: Get the diagnostic health report of the node. @return diagnostic health report of the node

method.name: getLastHealthReportTime
method.doc: Get the last timestamp at which the health report was received. @return last timestamp at which the health report was received

class.doc:
{@code NodeHealthStatus} is a summary of the health status of the node.

It includes information such as:

  • An indicator of whether the node is healthy, as determined by the health-check script.
  • The previous time at which the health status was reported.
  • A diagnostic report on the health status.
@see NodeReport @see ApplicationClientProtocol#getClusterNodes(org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.protocolrecords.GetClusterNodesRequest)

package.doc: Server records.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.api.records.impl.pb
package.doc: Server records PB implementations.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.federation.policies.dao
package.doc: DAO objects for serializing/deserializing policy configurations.

class.name: RequestType
class.doc: Easier classification of request types for logging metrics.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.scheduler
package.doc: Utility classes used for Scheduling.

package.name: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.uam

class.name: UnmanagedAMPoolManager

method.name: serviceStop
method.doc: Normally we should finish all applications before stop. If there are still UAMs running, force kill all of them. Do parallel kill because of performance reasons.

method.name: createAndRegisterNewUAM
launchUAM
reAttachUAM
createUAM
registerApplicationMaster
allocateAsync
finishApplicationMaster
shutDownConnections
shutDownConnections
method.doc:
Shutdown all UAM clients without killing them in YarnRM.
@throws YarnException if fails

method.name: getAllUAMIds
method.doc:
Get the id of all running UAMs.
@return uamId set

method.name: hasUAMId
getAMRMClientRelayer
batchFinishApplicationMaster

class.doc: A service that manages a pool of UAM managers in {@link UnmanagedApplicationManager}.

class.name: UnmanagedApplicationManager

constructor.name: UnmanagedApplicationManager
constructor.type: org.apache.hadoop.conf.Configuration, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ApplicationId, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, boolean, java.lang.String, org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ApplicationSubmissionContext

method.name: launchUAM
reAttachUAM
registerApplicationMaster
finishApplicationMaster
forceKillApplication
allocateAsync
shutDownConnections
method.doc: Shutdown this UAM client, without killing the UAM in the YarnRM side.

method.name: getAppId
method.doc:
Returns the application id of the UAM.
@return application id of the UAM

method.name: getAMRMClientRelayer
method.doc:
Returns the rmProxy relayer of this UAM.
@return rmProxy relayer of the UAM

method.name: createRMProxy
initializeUnmanagedAM
getUAMToken

class.doc: UnmanagedApplicationManager is used to register unmanaged application and negotiate for resources from resource managers. An unmanagedAM is an AM that is not launched and managed by the RM. Allocate calls are handled asynchronously using {@link AsyncCallback}.

class.name: LeveldbIterator

constructor.name: LeveldbIterator
constructor.doc:
Create an iterator for the specified database.
@param db database.

constructor.name: LeveldbIterator
LeveldbIterator
constructor.doc:
Create an iterator using the specified underlying DBIterator.
@param iter DB Iterator.

method.name: seek
seekToFirst
method.doc: Repositions the iterator so is is at the beginning of the Database.

method.name: seekToLast
method.doc: Repositions the iterator so it is at the end of of the Database.

method.name: hasNext
method.doc: Returns true if the iteration has more elements.

method.name: next
peekNext
hasPrev
method.doc: @return true if there is a previous entry in the iteration.

method.name: prev
method.doc: @return the previous element in the iteration and rewinds the iteration.

method.name: peekPrev
method.doc: @return the previous element in the iteration, without rewinding the iteration.

method.name: remove
method.doc: Removes from the database the last element returned by the iterator.

method.name: close
method.doc: Closes the iterator.

class.doc: A wrapper for a DBIterator to translate the raw RuntimeExceptions that can be thrown into DBExceptions.

method.name: getAllocatedResources
